mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 expands corporate earnings expectations, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDPC1 expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE drops below 5.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, driving consumer spending and equity earnings higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 5.5% in more than 40% of instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL compress real yields and increase discount rates, forcing equity multiples to contract over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations where both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals manufacturing sector momentum, which leads equity prices higher in the short term as supply chain efficiency boosts profit margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns during post-shock recovery periods (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST indicates weakening consumer confidence and credit conditions, reducing forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of cases following a >5% monthly HOUST decline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in PAYEMS and a declining UNRATE confirms broad-based labor market strength, driving sustained equity inflows over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal does not outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 45% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS reflect expanding consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across all available data points.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt 25-basis-point monthly hike in FEDFUNDS signals aggressive policy tightening, triggering immediate equity risk-off positioning over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following a >0.25% monthly FEDFUNDS increase.
